title: Split a Subnet for Multi-AZ
description:
## Carve a /24 Into /26s

You have a **`/24`** subnet and need to split it into the **largest equal subnets** that still give you **at least 4** non-overlapping ranges (one per AZ).

**Your task:** determine how many **`/26`** subnets a **`/24`** yields, and write just that number to **`/root/azsubnets.txt`**.

> 💡 /24 → /26 is 2 bits, so 2^2 subnets. Verify with Python's `ipaddress`.

hints.title: Subnet the network
hints.body: subnets(new_prefix=26) on a /24 enumerates the /26 blocks; count them.

validation.description: Checks that /root/azsubnets.txt contains the number of /26s in a /24 (4).
